name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Today, I spoke on the House floor for the first time to oppose the impeachment of President Trump. Watch my speech here. Like, and retweet below.  https://t.co/PfW5UZp8dJ

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: This impeachment effort ignored the normal procedure of allowing the President’s team a chance to defend him through hearings. I wish Speaker Pelosi had exercised the urgency she’s used in bringing this vote to the floor on negotiating COVID relief with Republicans last fall.

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Instead of further dividing our country, we should be focusing on a peaceful transition of power and on the many issues that need immediate solutions, not on impeaching a President who has one week left in office.

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: I strongly oppose today’s vote to impeach President Trump. Impeachment is not the appropriate response to last week’s inexcusable violence. It only serves to deepen the divide in our country and distract from the pressing issues facing all Americans.

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Thank you @SteveScalise for this great op-ed. I agree, we all must always condemn political violence and violent rhetoric, and not just when it fits our politics. Both the riots over the summer and the attack on the Capitol last week were wrong.  https://t.co/GQqQCZloxA

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Happy National Pharmacist Day! @Commpharmacy @pharmacists @amcporg  https://t.co/LtlxBKs9oe

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: I strongly oppose measures to invoke the 25th Amendment and impeach President Trump. Read my full statement below:  https://t.co/4uW0dpyXru

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Yesterday I met with Jerry Caldwell, General Manager @BMSupdates. We discussed this year’s plan for races & programs, and how BMS donated space to the Sullivan County Health Dept. for COVID vaccine administration. I will do all I can to make sure we can safely enjoy races soon.  https://t.co/82KdJHJN2R

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Today I visited the Sullivan County Health Department’s vaccine distribution at the Bristol Dragway where 1,000 doses were being distributed today. Thank you to everyone who is working to make sure East Tennesseans receive their COVID vaccine.  https://t.co/Bh7d5ZdbI6

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: I’m proudly displaying this sign at my office to show my gratitude to the brave men and women of the U.S. Capitol Police. These heroes’ actions last week saved many lives. God bless them and their loved ones.  https://t.co/hZ1sk3DyoQ

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: It was great to virtually meet with Erwin Alderman Baker and Unicoi County Mayor Evely. Thank you both for your leadership and I look forward to serving the people of Unicoi County alongside you.  https://t.co/KA5Fd6Tg5Y

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: @RepChuck Praying for a quick recovery!

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Thank you to all of the brave officers in law enforcement for your service and for protecting us. Now more than ever, we need to stand by our men and women in blue. May God bless you all!  https://t.co/Un6Gn5r6Js

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Twitter’s permanent suspension of President Trump is beyond disturbing. Instead of fostering open debate, this move will deepen the divide in this country. Unacceptable does not even begin to describe this move.  https://t.co/Fn7LkVBKSR

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: It is heartbreaking to hear that Officer Brian Sicknick passed away following Wednesday’s senseless violence. May God bless him and his family. Please join me in praying for his loved ones and for all of our brave law enforcement officers.

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Twitter thinks it’s ok to ban @realDonaldTrump but allows this tweet from Iran’s supreme leader that’s clearly inciting violence against America to stand? This is not acceptable!

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Sadly, this is just the latest example of big tech silencing conservatives. This is unacceptable and should not happen. Our society thrives when all opinions are heard and debated, not when many are silenced.

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Big tech should not censor the leader of the free world and restrict his ability to communicate directly to the American people, especially during a critical moment in our nation such as right now. Facebook and Twitter should restore @realDonaldTrump’s accounts immediately!

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: And to the brave men and women in the Capitol Police who protected us today- God bless you all, and thank you for your service. (3/3)

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: To my fellow Americans who are frustrated with the election results, we must let the objection process play out peacefully. We must never resort to violence to accomplish our objectives- that is contrary to how we do things in America. (2/3)

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Every American has the right to peacefully protest. What happened today was unacceptable. Those who broke into the Capitol and attacked our law enforcement should face the full consequences of the law. (1/3)

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: As Americans, we are better than the violence occurring today on Capitol Hill. We all have our first amendment right to peacefully protest the certification of election results, and should exercise it when warranted. Violence like this is never acceptable

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: "Rules for thee but not for me" must have been @SpeakerPelosi's year book quote

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: I’m proud to join @RepMikeJohnson in the fight for election integrity today!

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Fellow Tennesseans: check out this new tool that will help you determine when you’re eligible to receive the COVID-19 vaccine. Thank you, @GovBillLee, for unveiling this helpful new tool today.  https://t.co/yoZTnOpt7i

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: I’m excited to be a part of this historic group of freshmen Republicans and look forward to working with my colleagues on common sense conservative solutions to issues facing East Tennessee and our nation.  https://t.co/cO7P5HDM26

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: On Wednesday, I will stand up for election integrity and support an objection to the certification of the Electoral College vote for the states that failed to count all legal ballots. Read my full statement below ⬇️  https://t.co/qgnyfRbBF7

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Thank you! My staff and I look forward to working with you on higher education issues.

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Speaker Pelosi’s radical rules package runs contrary to American values by limiting debate, restricting free speech and banning the use of gendered terms like mother or father. As a proud mother, grandmother and American, I will be voting NO.  https://t.co/0VMIPvGuCF

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: I’m honored to stand here with my fellow freshmen Republican women and am excited to be a part of the fight for our shared conservative values.  https://t.co/uXqVhlpzbr

name: Rep. Diana Harshbarger
tweet: Thank you @DrPhilRoe for your dedicated service to East Tennessee, you left some big shoes to fill. Enjoy your retirement!  https://t.co/eutrVqB3CN
